heading: Broadcasting lottery information
label: 1304 Broadcasting lottery information
text: Whoever broadcasts by means of any radio or television station for which a license is required by any law of the United States, or whoever, operating any such station, knowingly permits the broadcasting of, any advertisement of or information concerning any lottery, gift enterprise, or similar scheme, offering prizes dependent in whole or in part upon lot or chance, or any list of the prizes drawn or awarded by means of any such lottery, gift enterprise, or scheme, whether said list contains any part or all of such prizes, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than one year, or both.Each day’s broadcasting shall constitute a separate offense.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on , U.S.C., 1940 ed., Telegraphs, Telephones, and Radiotelegraphs (, ).section 316 of title 47June 19, 1934, ch. 652, § 31648 Stat. 1088Words “upon conviction thereof” were deleted as surplusage since punishment can be imposed only after a conviction.Minor changes were made in phraseology.
Amendments1994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $1,000”.Pub. L. 103–3221988— inserted “or television” after “radio” in first sentence.Pub. L. 100–625

Effective Date of 1988 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub.
